sn22.150:1.1:
1
At Sāvatthī. "Bhikkhus, when what exists, by clinging to what, do pleasure and pain arise internally?"
sn22.150:1.2: "Venerable sir, our teachings are rooted in the Blessed One … ."
sn22.150:1.3: "When there is form, bhikkhus, by clinging to form, pleasure and pain arise internally. When there is feeling … perception … volitional formations … consciousness, by clinging to consciousness, pleasure and pain arise internally.
sn22.150:2.2: "What do you think, bhikkhus, is form permanent or impermanent?"
sn22.150:2.4: "Is what is impermanent suffering or happiness?"
sn22.150:2.6: "But without clinging to what is impermanent, suffering, and subject to change, could pleasure and pain arise internally?"
sn22.150:3.2: "Is feeling … perception … volitional formations … consciousness permanent or impermanent?
sn22.150:4.2: … But without clinging to what is impermanent, suffering, and subject to change, could pleasure and pain arise internally?"
sn22.150:5.3: "Seeing thus … He understands: ‘ … there is no more for this state of being.’"
